title: hebe ciliolata
intro: Hebe ciliolata, also known as the Alpine Hebe, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hebe ciliolata include its potential to reduce fever, ease digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Hebe ciliolata can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal infusions, often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
